title: fix(ui): handle missing failure callback in job-queue-poll
body:
### Summary
The job queue polling code in `job-queue-poll.js` assumes that a `callbackfail` function is always provided when calling `queueUpdateCheck`. However, some components (like `scheduledBootstrapSuccess` in the License Browser) don't pass a failure callback. When a job fails (e.g. from version check failure at the scheduler), it calls `callbackfail(jqId)` which throws `TypeError: callbackfail is not a function`, causing the page to crash and freeze completely.

### Fix
This PR implements two fixes:
1. **Defensive handling (`job-queue-poll.js`)**: Adds `typeof` checks before invoking callbacks. If no failure callback is provided, it falls back to `location.reload()` so the page doesn't freeze and reflects the previous state/licenses applied.
2. **Proper Error Feedback (`change-license-common.js`)**: Updates the `queueUpdateCheck` calls to pass a failure callback that displays a red alert banner saying "job failed (see job #X)" instead of silently reloading or crashing.

### Related Issues
Fixes #1595

*Note: This is a revised implementation following the closure of PR #3253, specifically addressing the maintainer's feedback that logging to the console is unhelpful for actual users. This ensures the user either sees a visible error banner or has the page reload safely.*

title: Fix memory leak in add2license_ref by ensuring PQclear() on error paths
body:
## Description

Fix a memory leak in the nomos agent by ensuring `PQclear()` is called on all error paths in `add2license_ref()`.

Previously, when `fo_checkPQresult()` returned an error, the function exited without freeing the `PGresult`, causing a memory leak detected by Valgrind.

---

### Changes

- Added `PQclear(result)` before returning in error paths inside `add2license_ref()`
- Fixed both occurrences where early return skipped memory cleanup
- Ensured proper handling of `PGresult` to avoid memory leaks

---

## How to test

1. Start Fossology using Docker:
   ```bash
   docker compose up -d

title: Valgrind reports uninitialised value usage and small memory leak in nomos during normal scan
body: Running the installed nomos agent under Valgrind inside the official FOSSology Docker environment consistently reports: - multiple Conditional jump or move depends on uninitialised value(s) warnings - one definite memory leak (6 bytes definitely lost) - one possible leak (32 bytes possibly lost) The scan does complete successully, but these warnings appear while executing normally. ### Steps to Reproduce - Start FOSSology using Docker: ```bash docker compose up -d ``` - Enter web container: ```bash docker exec -it fossology-web-1 bash ``` - Create test files: ```bash mkdir -p /tmp/repro echo "MIT License" > /tmp/repro/short.txt python3 -c "print('A'*50000)" > /tmp/repro/long.txt printf "GPL\n%s\nBSD\n" "$(python3 -c 'print("B"*20000)')" > /tmp/repro/mixed.txt ``` - Run normally: ```bash /usr/local/share/fossology/nomos/agent/nomos /tmp/repro/* ``` - Run under Valgrind: ```bash valgrind --leak-check=full --track-origins=yes /usr/local/share/fossology/nomos/agent/nomos /tmp/repro/* ``` **Output:**

title: Fix: Handle empty/null delimiter in LicenseCsvImport
body:
## Description

This PR fixes an issue where `substr()` was used on user-provided delimiter input without validation.  
When an empty string or null value is passed, `substr()` returns `false` instead of a valid string, leading to unexpected behavior in CSV parsing.

---

### Changes

- Added validation for empty, null, and non-string delimiter inputs
- Introduced default fallback delimiter `','` for invalid inputs
- Replaced `substr()` with safer string indexing (`$delimiter[0]`)
- Ensured consistent string output for delimiter handling

---

## How to test

1. Run FOSSology locally using Docker:
   ```bash
   docker compose up

title: fix(proxy): keep UploadTreeProxy view names unique and reuse named params
body:
## Summary
This is a clean, focused follow-up for #3378.

A previous attempt existed in #3379, but that PR was closed without being merged and maintainers asked for the correct isolated changes. This PR keeps the scope limited to the two `UploadTreeProxy` bugs from #3378 and adds regression coverage for them.

When `UploadTreeProxy` built a default DB view name, it accidentally discarded the option-specific suffix generated by `createUploadTreeViewQuery()`. That meant different proxy instances could collapse onto the same prepared statement / view name. This patch preserves the generated suffix and normalizes it so names like `lft,rgt` remain SQL-safe.

The second issue was in named parameter reuse. `addParamAndGetExpr()` checked for existing parameter keys, but new values were appended without preserving those keys, so the reuse path never actually worked. This patch stores parameters by name, reuses the intended placeholders, and keeps `countMaskedNonArtifactChildren()` compatible with the keyed parameter map.

## Tests
- Added regression checks for generated default view names
- Added regression checks for named parameter reuse across combined filters
- Added coverage for `countMaskedNonArtifactChildren()` with keyed params
- Ran `HOME=/tmp php -d xdebug.mode=off src/vendor/bin/phpunit --configuration src/phpunit.xml src/lib/php/Proxy/test/UploadTreeProxyTest.php`
- Result: `OK (20 tests, 31 assertions)`

Closes #3378

title: fix(reuser): support multiple upload reuse selections
body: ## Description When selecting multiple uploads in the UI, only one upload was processed for reuse. This occurred because the UI was sending only a single value to the backend, as `uploadToReuseSelectorName` was not defined as an array. Closes: #1271 ### Changes - `agent_reuser.html.twig`: Changed `uploadToReuseSelectorName` to an array to support multiple selections. - `agent-reuser.php`: Added logic to handle multiple uploads. ## Screenshots ### Before https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1b004a9e-544b-4e00-8083-d5d05752d72e ### After https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/da770155-c696-49ba-b301-04ceaada361c ## How to test - Select multiple uploads for reuse and verify that multiple items are processed in jobs. - Execute the following query: ```sql SELECT * FROM upload_reuse WHERE upload_fk = ; ```

title: test(api): extend UserController coverage for token and user management endpoints
body:
Relates to #1590

This adds test coverage for `createRestApiToken()` and `getTokens()` in `UserControllerTest`, two methods that had no tests before. For `createRestApiToken()` I've covered the success path along with the three exception cases — duplicate token key (HTTP 429), duplicate token name (HTTP 409), and invalid value (HTTP 400). For `getTokens()` I've added tests for active tokens, expired tokens, and invalid type (HTTP 400). Mock setup for `user_edit` and `manage-token` plugins has been added to `setUp()` to support these tests.

## New test methods added
- `testCreateRestApiTokenSuccess()`
- `testCreateRestApiTokenDuplicateKey()`
- `testCreateRestApiTokenDuplicateName()`
- `testCreateRestApiTokenInvalidValue()`
- `testGetTokensActive()`
- `testGetTokensExpired()`
- `testGetTokensInvalidType()`

## How to test
From the `src/` directory:
```shell
php vendor/bin/phpunit -c www/ui_tests/api/tests.xml --testsuite "Fossology PhpUnit REST API"
```

title: git push origin fix/memory-bloat-safe-lookupFix/memory bloat safe lookup
body:
<!-- SPDX-FileCopyrightText: © Fossology contributors

     SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-only
-->

<!-- Please refer to CONTRIBUTING.md (https://github.com/fossology/fossology/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
before creating the pull request to make sure you follow all the standards. -->

## Description

This PR fixes a memory inefficiency issue in the Scancode agent caused by the use of the map index operator (`operator[]`) on `fileIdsMapReverse`.

In C++, using `map[key]` for lookup can lead to implicit insertion of a new key with a default value if the key does not already exist. During large scans with many files, this behavior can cause the map to grow unnecessarily, leading to memory bloat.

## Changes

- Removed the use of `fileIdsMapReverse[file.getFileName()] = file.getId();`
- Ensured that no unintended insertions occur in `fileIdsMapReverse`
- Preserved existing functionality of `fileIdsMap` mapping

## Impact

- Prevents unnecessary growth of the reverse map
- Improves memory efficiency during large file scans
- Maintains existing behavior without introducing new logic

## How to Test

1. Run a scan using the Scancode agent on a large dataset
2. Observe that the reverse map (`fileIdsMapReverse`) does not grow unexpectedly
3. Ensure that file name to ID mapping still works correctly

## Notes

This change is minimal and focuses only on removing unsafe map usage without altering the core logic.

Closes #3441

title: Empty/Short String Handling in CSV Import
body:
### Summary
There is a bug in `LicenseCsvImport.php` where `substr()` is used on user-provided input without validating for empty strings. When an empty string is passed, `substr()` returns `false` instead of a string, which leads to unexpected behavior in CSV parsing.

### Affected File
- `src/lib/php/Application/LicenseCsvImport.php`

Input | substr Result | Impact
-- | -- | --
"" | false | fgetcsv() fails or behaves unexpectedly
null | false | Same issue

### Problematic Code
```php
public function setDelimiter($delimiter=',')
{
  $this->delimiter = substr($delimiter,0,1);
}

title: fix(upload): prevent orphan DB records when file move fails
body:
### Problem
During multi-file uploads, a database record is created before the file is successfully moved to disk. If the file move operation fails, the database entry remains without a corresponding file, leading to orphaned upload records.

### Root Cause
The function JobAddUpload() is called before verifying successful file storage.

### Fix
Added cleanup logic to delete the database record when file movement fails.

### Impact
- Prevents database inconsistency
- Ensures upload records always correspond to actual files

### Testing
- Simulated file upload failure
- Verified that no orphan records remain in the database

Closes #<3483>

title: Potential inconsistency in multi-file upload handling may lead to orphaned records
body:
During multi-file uploads, each file appears to be registered in the database (via JobAddUpload or similar logic) before the file is successfully moved to its final storage location.

If the file transfer fails (for example due to permission issues, invalid paths, or storage limitations), this may result in database records that do not have corresponding files on disk.

This could lead to inconsistencies between the database and filesystem.

Possible scenario:
1. Upload multiple files in a single request
2. One file is successfully stored
3. Another file fails during move_uploaded_file()
4. Database record may already exist for the failed file

Expected behavior:
Database records should only be created after successful file storage, or appropriate rollback/cleanup should be performed if file storage fails.

Suggested improvements:
- Stage and validate files before inserting database records, or
- Add rollback logic to remove database entries when file storage fails

Impact:
This could potentially affect downstream scanning jobs if records exist without corresponding files.

Note:
This observation is based on code inspection of the upload handling flow and may need confirmation from maintainers.

Reference:
src/www/ui/page/UploadFilePage.php (handleUpload function)

title: CI: add workflow to cleanup PR caches on close
body:
<!-- SPDX-FileCopyrightText: © Fossology contributors

     SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-only
-->

<!-- Please refer to CONTRIBUTING.md (https://github.com/fossology/fossology/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
before creating the pull request to make sure you follow all the standards. -->

## Description

This pull request introduces a GitHub Actions workflow to automatically clean up runner caches scoped to a pull request when it is closed or merged. This prevents orphaned caches from accumulating and hitting the repository's cache limit, ensuring that caches for active and important branches (like `master`) are not prematurely evicted.

### Changes

- Added [.github/workflows/cleanup-caches.yml](cci:7://file:///home/atheus/fossology/.github/workflows/cleanup-caches.yml:0:0-0:0) which triggers on `pull_request` close events.
- Uses the `gh` API via the GitHub CLI to safely fetch and delete cache keys bounded perfectly to the closed PR's branch reference.

## How to test

1. Push this branch and verify that GitHub Actions syntax passes correctly.
2. Open a test Pull Request that generates some cache (e.g. via the standard Docker build workflow).
3. Close the Pull Request.
4. Verify in the repository's **Actions -> Caches** tab that the cache keys associated with that ephemeral branch were automatically purged while keeping other caches unaffected.

fixes #3479

title: Add GitHub Actions workflow to clean up PR caches on close
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently, caches generated by GitHub Actions workflows for Pull Requests (using `actions/cache` or `docker/build-push-action` cache-to/from features) are left behind even after the PR is closed or merged. These orphaned caches accumulate and consume the repository's cache storage limit, causing active and important caches (like the `master` branch caches) to be evicted prematurely, slowing down builds.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add a GitHub Actions workflow (e.g., [cleanup-caches.yml](cci:7://file:///home/atheus/fossology/.github/workflows/cleanup-caches.yml:0:0-0:0)) that automatically deletes runner caches scoped to a pull request when it is closed or merged.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Allowing caches to be deleted naturally when they hit the global repository limits (the current behavior). However, this impacts the speed of subsequent builds as legitimate active caches from the base branch are cleared out.

**Additional context**
This is a standard workflow highly recommended in the GitHub Actions documentation for repository hygiene:
[GitHub Docs: Force deleting caches](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/caching-dependencies-to-speed-up-workflows#force-deleting-caches)

title: fix(nomos): preserve getline base pointer during line trimming
body:
## Description

Fixed undefined behavior in `nomos` line trimming. Currently the heap buffer returned by `getline()`  is modified directly during leading whitespace removal. This shifts the original pointer and can result in invalid memory deallocation when `free()` is called later.

### Changes
- A separate working pointer is initialized to preserve the original `getline()` allocation.
- Whitespace trimming updated to operate on the working pointer instead of owned buffer pointer.
- Added bounds checking before trailing whitespace trimming.
- Used `(unsigned char)` casts in `isspace()` calls for safe character handling.
- Passed the trimmed working pointer to `processFile()` while keeping the original pointer for `free()`.

title: fix(api): null safety and wrong status codes in GroupController member endpoints
body:
## What's this about

While going through the GroupController code I noticed a few bugs in the group member management endpoints that could cause hard crashes in PHP 8 and some HTTP responses that don't quite follow REST conventions. This PR fixes all of them.

---

### PHP 8 crash when username doesn't exist (V2)

In V2 mode, `deleteGroupMember`, `addMember`, and `changeUserPermission` accept a username in the URL path and look it up via `getUserByName()`. The problem is they were chaining straight into `['user_pk']` without ever checking if the lookup actually found anything:

\`\`\`php
// before — crashes if user doesn't exist
$user_pk = intval($this->restHelper->getUserDao()->getUserByName($args['userPathParam'])['user_pk']);
\`\`\`

When `getUserByName` returns `null` (user not found), PHP 8 throws a `TypeError` on the array access instead of returning null quietly like PHP 7 did. The caller ends up with a 500 instead of a meaningful 404.

Fixed by storing the result first, checking for null, and throwing an `HttpNotFoundException` with a clear message before touching any keys.

### Another crash hiding behind a guard in `changeUserPermission`

There was a similar issue with `getSingleRow` in `changeUserPermission`:

\`\`\`php
// before — the empty() check below never runs if getSingleRow returns null
$group_user_member_pk = $dbManager->getSingleRow(...)['group_user_member_pk'];

if (empty($group_user_member_pk)) { ... }
\`\`\`

In PHP 8, if the row doesn't exist, the chained key access crashes before the `empty()` guard is ever reached. Split it into two lines so the guard actually does its job.

### Status codes that didn't match what the API was actually doing

- `deleteGroupMember` was returning **200 (OK)** even though the response message literally says "User *will be* removed" — if the operation is deferred/async, **202 (Accepted)** is the right code.
- `addMember` was returning **200 (OK)** after creating a brand new group membership. Creating something should be **201 (Created)**.

---

## Changes

| File | What changed |
|---|---|
| `GroupController.php` | Null guards in `deleteGroupMember`, `addMember`, `changeUserPermission`; split `getSingleRow` chain in `changeUserPermission`; status codes 200→202 and 200→201 |
| `GroupControllerTest.php` | Updated existing tests to match the new status codes; added 5 new tests covering the 404 path and correct 202 for `deleteGroupMember` |

## Tests

All 635 PHPUnit tests pass locally. The new tests cover:
- `deleteGroupMember` → user not found → 404 (V2)
- `addMember` → user not found → 404 (V2)
- `changeUserPermission` → user not found → 404 (V2)
- `deleteGroupMember` → success → 202 (both V1 and V2)

title: feat: implement real-time dashboard sync for audit decisions
body:
<!-- SPDX-FileCopyrightText: © Fossology contributors

     SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-only
-->

<!-- Please refer to CONTRIBUTING.md (https://github.com/fossology/fossology/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
before creating the pull request to make sure you follow all the standards. -->
### Description

This PR improves synchronization between audit decisions and dashboard statistics by introducing an immediate refresh hook after a successful audit update.

### Problem

The dashboard relies on a cached `dashboard_stats` table, which is not automatically updated when audit decisions are modified. This leads to stale "Total Pending" counts until a manual refresh or system restart occurs.

### Solution

This PR introduces a **post-save synchronization step in the AuditController**, ensuring that whenever an audit decision is saved:

* The database function `refresh_dashboard_stats(uploadId)` is triggered
* The dashboard statistics are recalculated immediately for that upload

### Why this approach

* Keeps changes minimal and localized to the audit flow
* Avoids modifying existing database schema or triggers
* Provides immediate consistency for UI-driven audit actions

### Limitation (Known)

This approach is currently scoped to API-driven updates.
A future improvement could move this logic to the database layer (e.g., triggers) to ensure full system-wide consistency.

### Result

* Eliminates manual refresh requirement in typical UI workflow
* Reduces perceived lag in audit-to-dashboard updates

### How to Verify

1. Open dashboard and note "Total Pending"
2. Clear a finding in audit view
3. Refresh dashboard

✔ Count updates immediately after action.
#3460

title: Add Duplicate File Detection Using SHA256 Hash During File Upload
body:
**Description**

Currently, the upload functionality allows the same file to be uploaded multiple times without detecting duplicates.
When identical files are uploaded repeatedly, the system processes them as separate uploads and schedules additional scans. This can lead to unnecessary storage usage and redundant scanning.

**Steps to Reproduce**
1.Navigate to the Upload page
2.Upload a file
3.Upload the same file again

**Current Behaviour**
The system accepts the file as a new upload and processes it again.

**Expected Behaviour**
The system should detect if the uploaded file already exists and notify the user instead of creating a duplicate upload.

**Possible Improvement**
Duplicate detection could be implemented by comparing file hashes during upload.

title: fix(ui): prevent duplicate API calls on repeated dashboard/file view loads
body:
**Description**
While navigating between the dashboard and file view, I observed that certain API endpoints are being triggered multiple times unnecessarily.
This can happen due to repeated component mounts or unintended re-renders, leading to duplicate network requests for the same data.

**Problem**
Redundant API calls increase backend load
Slows down UI performance
Causes inefficient data fetching

**Proposed Solution**
Identify components where duplicate API calls occur
Add appropriate guards to ensure API requests are triggered only when required
Prevent repeated calls during unnecessary re-renders

**Expected Outcome**
Each API is called only once per required lifecycle
Improved performance and reduced network overhead

**Additional Context**

This can be verified using the browser’s Network tab, where multiple identical requests are triggered during navigation.

title: Unsafe use of sprintf in buckets agent may cause buffer overflow
body:
While reviewing the `buckets` agent (`src/buckets/agent/buckets.c`), I found multiple uses of `sprintf` to construct SQL queries and strings.

Example:

`sprintf(sqlbuf, "select bucketpool_pk from bucketpool where bucketpool_pk=%d and active='Y'", bucketpool_pk);`

`sprintf` does not enforce buffer size limits, which can potentially lead to buffer overflows if the formatted output exceeds the allocated buffer.

### Impact
- Potential buffer overflow vulnerabilities
- Reduced memory safety
- Inconsistent use of safe string handling functions across the codebase

### Suggested Improvement

Replace `sprintf` with `snprintf`, which ensures that writes do not exceed buffer boundaries.

Example fix:

`snprintf(sqlbuf, sizeof(sqlbuf),
         "select bucketpool_pk from bucketpool where bucketpool_pk=%d and active='Y'",
         bucketpool_pk);`

### Scope

File: `src/buckets/agent/buckets.c`
Multiple occurrences of `sprintf` can be safely replaced with `snprintf`

### Notes

This change does not affect functionality but improves security and robustness.

I want to work on this issue and submit a PR. Please let me know if this approach is acceptable or if there are preferred guidelines for string handling in this project.

title: feat(copyrightHist): add functionility to group select items using Sh…
body:
…ift key

<!-- SPDX-FileCopyrightText: © Fossology contributors

     SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-only
-->

<!-- Please refer to CONTRIBUTING.md (https://github.com/fossology/fossology/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
before creating the pull request to make sure you follow all the standards. -->

## Description

Extend the select all functionality to detect grouped select using shift key from keyboard.

### Changes

Identify the min and max current rows using math and calculate the distance to select rest.

## How to test

* Go to copyright hist page.
* For activated copyrights table select any one copyright row in the middle of the table.
* Now press shift and select n numbered row.
* All the rows in between shall get selected.

Note: the functionality is not implemented for selection of <td> to avoid duplicate selection of single row.

title: Fix(ui): rename "Copy" action to "Alias" in content_move (#613)
body: ### Description Renamed the "Copy" action to "Alias" in the content_move UI. The current "Copy" functionality actually creates an alias to the same folder instead of creating a new copy. Renaming it to "Alias" better reflects the behavior and avoids confusion for users. ### Changes - Updated UI label from "Copy" to "Alias" in the content_move page. ### Issue Fixes #613

title: fix(buckets): prevent NULL dereference when parsing scheduler input
body:
The buckets agent parses scheduler input using strtok_r and atoi.

If malformed input is received (missing value after bppk= or upk=),
strtok_r may return NULL, which is then passed to atoi(). This results
in undefined behavior, which may cause a crash.

This patch adds validation before converting tokens to integers to
prevent NULL dereference and improve robustness.

title: feat: implement real-time dashboard sync for audit decisions
body: **Description** This pull request introduces real-time synchronization between the Audit API and the Dashboard statistics. Previously, when a clearing expert saved an audit decision, the dashboard "Total Pending" statistics did not update immediately and required manual refresh or container restart. This change ensures that whenever a decision is successfully saved, the dashboard statistics for the corresponding upload are refreshed automatically. Closes #3460 **Changes** -Added a post-save trigger in AuditController.php after saveDecision() execution. -Implemented refreshUploadStats() in DashboardDao.php. The new method calls the PostgreSQL function refresh_dashboard_stats($uploadId) to update dashboard statistics.
